requirements.summary: Build main dashboard with 5 navigation buttons for Add Stock, Record Sale, View Inventory, Daily Sales Report, and Reset Inventory
acceptanceCriteria: All 5 buttons are visible on the home screen
Buttons are large enough for easy thumb-tap on a mobile screen
Tapping each button navigates to the correct flow or triggers password prompt where required

file_operations.description: Create main dashboard page component with 5 large, mobile-friendly navigation buttons: Add Stock, Record Sale, View Inventory, Daily Sales Report, Reset Inventory. Use full-width button layout with generous padding. Each button navigates to its respective route using React Router.
Configure React Router with routes for dashboard (/) and all feature screens. Import and wire Dashboard component as the home route.

requirements.summary: Build Add Stock flow with product selection, quantity buttons (+10, +20, +50), price selection (0.50, 0.60, 0.70 + manual input), and confirmation
acceptanceCriteria: User can complete the full add stock flow using only button taps (except optional manual price input)
All 5 products appear as selectable buttons
Quantity buttons show +10, +20, +50
Price buttons show 0.50, 0.60, 0.70 with an additional manual input field
Submitting calls the backend add stock function and shows a success message
User can navigate back to home after completion

file_operations.description: Create multi-step Add Stock flow page. Step 1: Display 5 product buttons (Marlboro Silver, Marlboro Gold, Parliament Silver, Parliament Platinium, Parliament Superslim). Step 2: Show quantity buttons (+10, +20, +50). Step 3: Show price buttons (0.50, 0.60, 0.70) and a manual numeric input field. Step 4: Confirmation screen. Call backend.addStock() with selected product name, quantity (as bigint), and price (as number). Display success message after backend call completes. Include back button in header.
Add route /add-stock that renders the AddStock page component.

requirements.summary: Build Record Sale flow with product selection, quantity buttons (-1, -2, -5, -10), selling price buttons (9, 10, 11, 12 AZN), and confirmation with error handling for insufficient stock
acceptanceCriteria: User can complete the full sale flow using only button taps
All 5 products appear as selectable buttons
Quantity buttons show -1, -2, -5, -10
Selling price buttons show 9 AZN, 10 AZN, 11 AZN, 12 AZN
If stock is insufficient, display a clear error message and do not submit
Successful sale shows a confirmation message and returns to home

file_operations.description: Create multi-step Record Sale flow page. Step 1: Display 5 product buttons. Step 2: Show quantity buttons (-1, -2, -5, -10). Step 3: Show selling price buttons (9 AZN, 10 AZN, 11 AZN, 12 AZN). Step 4: Confirmation. Call backend.recordSale() with product name, quantity (as positive bigint), selling price (as number), and timestamp (Date.now() as bigint). Catch backend errors for insufficient stock and display error message. Show success confirmation on completion. Include back button.
Add route /record-sale that renders the RecordSale page component.

requirements.summary: Build password-protected Inventory View screen showing all 5 products with current stock quantities
acceptanceCriteria: Password prompt appears before showing inventory
Correct password (gulserxan) grants access to the inventory list
Inventory shows all 5 products with their current stock quantities
Wrong password shows an error and does not reveal inventory
Password is validated client-side

file_operations.description: Create password-protected inventory view page. Show password input prompt on mount. Validate password === 'gulserxan' client-side. On correct password, fetch and display list of all 5 products with their current stock quantities (query backend for product data). Show error message for wrong password. Include back button.
Add route /view-inventory that renders the ViewInventory page component.

requirements.summary: Build password-protected Daily Sales Report screen showing today's sales summary per product and total revenue
acceptanceCriteria: Password prompt appears before showing the report
Correct password grants access to the daily report
Report shows each product sold today with quantity sold and revenue
Total revenue for the day is displayed
If no sales today, shows a clear 'No sales today' message
Wrong password shows an error

file_operations.description: Create password-protected daily sales report page. Show password input prompt. Validate password === 'gulserxan' client-side. On correct password, call backend.getTodaySales() which returns Array<[string, bigint, number]> (product name, total quantity sold, total revenue). Display list of products sold today with their quantities and revenue. Calculate and display total revenue for the day. Show 'No sales today' if array is empty. Show error for wrong password. Include back button.
Add route /daily-sales-report that renders the DailySalesReport page component.

requirements.summary: Build Admin Reset flow with password protection and confirmation dialog to reset all stock quantities to zero
acceptanceCriteria: Password prompt appears before showing reset option
Correct password reveals a confirmation dialog with a clear warning
User must confirm before the reset is executed
After reset, all product stock quantities show as 0
Wrong password shows an error and does not reset

file_operations.description: Create admin reset page with password protection. Step 1: Show password input, validate password === 'gulserxan' client-side. Step 2: On correct password, show confirmation dialog warning that all stock will be reset to zero. Step 3: On user confirmation, call backend.resetStocks(). Display success message after reset completes. Show error for wrong password. Include back button.
Add route /reset-inventory that renders the ResetInventory page component.

requirements.summary: Apply dark-themed visual design with amber accent, large mobile-friendly buttons, consistent header with back navigation, and mobile-first single-column layout
acceptanceCriteria: Dark theme is applied consistently across all screens
Buttons have large tap targets (minimum 48px height) with rounded corners
Amber/orange accent color is used for primary action buttons
Font size is large and readable on small mobile screens
All screens use a single-column mobile-first layout
Sub-screens have a back button to return to the dashboard

file_operations.description: Define dark theme CSS variables: near-black or very dark gray background (e.g., #0a0a0a or #1a1a1a), high-contrast white/light-gray text (#ffffff or #f5f5f5), and warm amber/orange accent color (e.g., #f59e0b or #fb923c) for primary actions. Set base font size large for mobile readability (16px minimum). Apply to :root and body.

Extend Tailwind theme to include custom dark background colors, amber accent color, and large button sizing utilities. Configure minimum button height (48px) and generous padding for mobile tap targets.

Create reusable Header component displaying app name 'Shop Inventory POS' and a back button (when not on dashboard). Use dark background and amber accent for app name. Include React Router navigation for back button.
Create Layout wrapper component with Header and main content area. Apply dark background, single-column mobile-first layout, and consistent padding. Use this layout in App.tsx to wrap all routes.
Wrap all routes with Layout component to apply consistent dark theme, header, and mobile-first structure across all pages.

Wire the generated shop logo icon (simple stylized cash register or shop bag silhouette on dark background with warm amber accent) into the Header component. Reference this file path in Header.tsx to display the logo next to the app name.
